summary: First full test of EPFF-INTEP blog. Backend: 11/11 pytest cases pass (stats, turmas, posts CRUD/filter/slug, auth, comments moderation flow, admin CRUD). Frontend: Home/Blog/Turmas/Sobre/PostDetail all load with expected content; 5 posts, 5 turma cards; /admin redirects unauth to /admin/login; admin login with seeded credentials works, Painel/Sair shown, all 3 tabs (Artigos/Turmas/Comentários) are clickable.

integration_issues.issue: Could not locate comment form inputs via standard selectors (name='author_name'/textarea name='message'/data-testid). Manual selectors may differ; backend comment POST is verified working via pytest, so likely just different field names. Recommend adding data-testid='comment-author', 'comment-message', 'comment-submit' for reliable automation.

action_items: Add data-testid attributes on PostDetail comment form (author name input, message textarea, submit button) to enable deterministic UI automation. Backend flow already verified.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py seed_admin correctly updates existing admin if password mismatches (good).
CORS allow_origins uses wildcard via env default '*' with allow_credentials=False — acceptable for public blog; tighten to domain list in prod.
admin_update_comment uses query param `approved: bool` (PUT /api/admin/comments/{id}?approved=true); works but a JSON body would be more conventional.
All endpoints properly exclude `_id` from Mongo responses.

success_rate.frontend: ~95% (comment form automation blocked by selector mismatch; visible flows all OK)

seed_data_creation: Startup seed creates 5 turmas and 5 posts when collections empty; verified via /api/stats=5/5
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/test_epff_api.py passes 11/11. For frontend comment submission UI automation, inspect PostDetail.jsx for actual input names or add data-testid hooks.
